category_title: Attention-seeking students without language disorders who mimic peers
minimal_context: The class is participating in a whole group discussion to introduce the vocabulary word 'declared' during an interactive read-aloud of The Day the Crayons Quit.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: We are reading The Day the Crayons Quit today. Let's talk about our new vocabulary word.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I want to be the helper! I can be the helper for the book!

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's see if anyone knows the word. Helen, what do you think it is?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Looking around at the other students) I know it! I have thoughts to share too!

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Helen just said the word is declared.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: We are reading The Day the Crayons Quit today. Let's talk about our new vocabulary word.

STUDENT: I want to be the helper! I can be the helper for the book!

TUTOR: Let's see if anyone knows the word. Helen, what do you think it is?

STUDENT: (Looking around at the other students) I know it! I have thoughts to share too!

TUTOR: Helen just said the word is declared.

STUDENT: Declared.

task.content: Introduction of the vocabulary word 'declared' during the interactive read-aloud of The Day the Crayons Quit
task.learning_goal: Building students' receptive and expressive vocabulary

context.materials: The Day the Crayons Quit book
context.time_context: During the introduction of the words

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: During the introduction of the words when she demonstrated a strong desire to share her thoughts spontaneously
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Yells out the vocabulary word without raising her hand to volunteer
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Seemed to long for peer attention

behavior_patterns.condition: Throughout the remainder of the read-aloud sessions
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Continued to seek attention for her thoughts, knowledge, and willingness to be the 'helper'
